skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: An object's average acceleration, in meters per second squared, is the change in the object's speed, in meters per second, divided by the time during which the change occurred, in seconds. A certain object's speed changes by 22 meters per second in 8 seconds. What is this object's average acceleration, in meters per second squared, during this time period?

skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage:
x + 47 = y
(x + 47) 2 = y
A solution to the given system of equations is (x, y). What is a possible value of x?

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage:
f(x) = 50 x + 8
The given function f estimates the distance a train has traveled, in kilometers, from a station in a certain city x hours after crossing the city border. What is the best interpretation of 8 in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Between the station and the city border, the train traveled an estimated total distance of 8 kilometers.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Between the statio n and the city border, the train traveled at an estimated speed of 8 kilometers per hour.

choices.label: C
choices.text: After crossing the city border, the train traveled at an estimated speed of 8 kilometers per hour.

choices.label: D
choices.text: After crossing the city border, the train traveled an estimated total distance of 8 kilometers.

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$\frac{1}{6}(x+2)-\frac{1}{5}(x+2)=-5$
What value of x is the solution to the given equation?

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: The function g is defined by g(x) = x - 18. What is the x -coordinate of the x-intercept of the graph of y= g(x) in the xy -plane?

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: A sum of 4,000 dollars was invested in a certificate of deposit (CD) that earns interest, and no other deposit or withdrawals were made after that. The function f(x)= 4,000(1+0.02) x gives the vale of the CD. In dollars, x years after the investment was initially made, where x $\le$ 5. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the statement “f(2) is approximately equal to 4,186.12" in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The value of the CD would be approximately 4,186.12 dollars if invested at 2% interest.

choices.label: B
choices.text: 4,186.12 days after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 2 times greater than its initial value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 4,186.12 dollars.

choices.label: D
choices.text: years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 4,186.12 dollars greater than its initial value.

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: Two students are playing a game. In the first round, Player 1 answers 33 questions, If an answer is correct, Player l earns l point, If an answer is incorrect, Player 2 will earn l point instead. The graph shows y = f(x), where y is the number of points Player 2 will earn when x is the number of points Player l earns. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the point (33,0) in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When Player 1 earns 33 points, Player 2 will earn 33 points.

choices.label: B
choices.text: When Player 1 earns 33 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.

choices.label: C
choices.text: When Player 1 earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 33 points.

choices.label: D
choices.text: When Player l earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.

skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: The manager of a gym selected a sample of 139 members at random to estimate the percentage of the gym's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased. From the survey, the manager estimates that 82% of the gym's members would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased, with an associated margin of error of 6.39%. If the survey is repeated with a random sample of 278 members and the results are calculated in the same way, which of the following will be the most likely effect of using the larger random sample compared to the smaller random sample?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The margin of error will be lower.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The margin of error will be higher.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The estimate of the percentage of the gym's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased will be lower.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The estimate of the percentage of the gym's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased will be higher.

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: Raheem bought 9 shirts that were each the same price, He used a coupon for \$54 off the entire purchase. The cost for the entire purchase after using the coupon was \$108. What was the original price, in dollars. for 1 shirt?

skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage:
The maximum temperature on April l, in degrees Fahrenheit (°F), was recorded each year at a certain weather station for l1 years. The histogram summarizes the recorded data set.
The temperature of 78.2 °F is removed from this data set to create a new data set of 10 temperatures.
Which of the following statements must be true?
I. The mean of the new data set is less than the mean of the original data set.
II. The median of the new data set is less than the median of the original data set.

passage: For the positive quantities h, j, and k, 94% of h is equivalent to 47% of j, and j is equivalent to 30% of k. What percentage of k is h? (Disregard the % sign when entering your answer.For example, if your answer is 39%, enter 39)

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: For the exponential function f, the value of f (1) is k, where k is a constant. Which of the following equivalent forms of the function f shows the value of k as the coefficient or the base?

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: In triangle PQR, the measure of angle P is (3 x + 5)°,the measure of angle Q is (2 x + 9)°, and the measure of angle R is (4 y + 5)°. If side QR is extended through point R to point S, and the measure of angle PRS is (x + y)°, what is the value of x + y?

passage: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. If a triangle has side lengths of 8 and 13, which inequality represents the possible lengths, x, of the third side of the triangle?

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: A right circular cone has a volume of 2,700 π cubic centimeters and the area of its base is 225π square centimeters. What is the slant height, in centimeters, of this cone?

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: A quadratic function models the height, in feet, of an object above the ground in terms of the time, in seconds, after the object was launched. According to the model, the object was launched from a height of 0 feet and reached its maximum height of 1,600 feet 10 seconds after it was launched. Based on the model, what was the height, in feet, of the object 15 seconds after it was launched?

test_name: June 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: The cost of renting a power washer is \$ 92 for the first day and \$46 for each additional day. Which of the following functions gives the cost C(d), in dollars, of renting the power washer for d days, where d is a positive integer?

passage: The function f is defined by f(x) = ax 2 + b x + c, where a, b, and c are constants. The graph of y= f(x) in the xy -plane passes through the points (9, 0) and (-2, 0). If a is an integer greater than 1, which of the following could be the value of a + b?
